title: haplopappus diplopappus var. struthionum
intro: Haplopappus diplopappus var. struthionum, commonly known as the antelope's horn, is a type of flowering plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of North America. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin irritations. The antelope's horn has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, and is also used to treat digestive problems and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water, or by using the roots to create a decoction, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
